skill.name: zentao-cli
skill.description: Use the zentao CLI to query and manage ZenTao records, including products, projects, stories, bugs, tasks, releases, tickets, users, and files.
skill.summary: Query, update, and manage ZenTao work items through the official zentao CLI workflow.
skill.author: easysoft

security_audit.summary: Most command-execution hits are Markdown examples for using zentao-cli, not executable skill code. Confirmed risks are external CLI execution, ZenTao authentication, local credential caching, and write or delete operations. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Installation and Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill tells users to install zentao-cli globally or run it through npx. This is expected, but it executes third-party code locally.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly instruct installation and execution of an external command-line tool. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the execution risk is real because the package runs locally.

medium_findings.title: Credential Handling and Local Credential Cache
medium_findings.description: The skill documents login with server URL, account, password, token, and local credential storage. Exposed local files could reveal ZenTao access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly documents password or token authentication and local credential caching. No malicious exfiltration is shown, but credential exposure risk is directly evidenced.

medium_findings.title: State-Changing and Destructive ZenTao Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill includes create, update, delete, resolve, close, start, and finish operations. It also shows --yes for delete confirmation bypass.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited examples directly perform writes and deletion. The skill also advises confirmation before writes, which lowers but does not remove operational risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The repository URL and sample ZenTao server URL are documentation references. No evidence found of unauthorized external data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in metadata and login examples. They are consistent with project documentation and do not show exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Positives From Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography and high-entropy alerts appear to come from front matter, Chinese text, and Markdown examples. No obfuscated payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found normal YAML front matter and Chinese documentation text at the flagged lines. The file is readable Markdown, not obfuscated code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Global Package Installation Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The installation examples run npm, bun, pnpm, or npx for zentao-cli. Users should verify the package source before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit installation paths for an external CLI. This is expected behavior, but it is a meaningful execution pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Cache Location Documented
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide states that credentials are cached in a hidden user configuration file. This is sensitive local filesystem storage.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact cache path is documented on the cited line. The finding is limited to local credential exposure risk, not active theft.

dangerous_patterns.title: Delete Confirmation Bypass Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide shows using --yes to skip delete confirmation in AI scenarios. This raises risk for accidental destructive actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly recommend --yes for deletion in AI use. The skill later advises confirmation before writes, so the concern is operational rather than malicious.

content.user_title: Manage ZenTao Work from the CLI
content.value_statement: Teams using ZenTao need fast access to project, bug, task, and release data without switching contexts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through safe zentao CLI workflows for reading and updating records.
actual_capabilities: List and inspect ZenTao products, projects, programs, executions, and users.
Create, update, delete, and transition stories, bugs, tasks, feedback, and tickets.
Filter, search, sort, paginate, and select fields from CLI results.
Use workspace context to avoid repeating product, project, and execution parameters.
Request Markdown output for users or JSON output for structured processing.
Guide login, token use, and common ZenTao CLI error recovery.
limitations: Requires zentao-cli to be installed or runnable with npx.
Requires valid ZenTao credentials and network access to a ZenTao server.
Does not validate business rules beyond what the ZenTao server enforces.
Write and delete actions can change production project data.

use_cases.title: Review active bugs for a product
use_cases.description: List high priority active bugs, select key fields, and summarize work that needs attention.

use_cases.title: Update tasks during a sprint
use_cases.description: Find execution tasks, start work, finish tasks, and record consumed effort through the CLI.

use_cases.title: Prepare project status reports
use_cases.description: Query projects, executions, stories, builds, and releases to assemble a concise status view.

prompt_templates.title: List Product Bugs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the zentao CLI to list bugs for product 1. Show id, title, severity, priority, status, and assignee.

prompt_templates.title: Find Urgent Active Bugs
prompt_templates.prompt: Find active bugs for product 1 where severity is 2 or lower and priority is 2 or lower. Sort the result by priority and severity.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Task After Confirmation
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a zentao task create command for execution 3 named Implement login validation. Assign it to user admin with estimate 4. Ask me to confirm before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Release Readiness Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Use zentao to review product 1 release readiness. Check active bugs, open stories, test tasks, builds, and planned releases. Summarize blockers and recommended next actions.

output_examples.input: Show active bugs for product 1 with high severity.
output_examples.output: A concise table of matching bugs with id, title, severity, priority, status, and assigned user, followed by a short risk summary.

output_examples.input: Create a new task in execution 2 for API documentation.
output_examples.output: A proposed task creation command and a confirmation request before any project data is changed.
A project status summary covering active executions, open tasks, unresolved bugs, and recent release items.

best_practices: Confirm every create, update, delete, and status transition before running it.
Use --pick, --filter, --sort, and pagination to keep results focused.
Use token or environment based authentication only in trusted local environments.
anti_patterns: Do not run delete commands with --yes unless the user explicitly confirmed the exact target.
Do not paste passwords or tokens into shared transcripts or logs.
Do not use broad --all queries when a scoped product, project, or execution query will work.

faq.question: Does this skill install ZenTao itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides use of zentao-cli against an existing ZenTao server.

faq.question: Can it make changes to ZenTao data?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports create, update, delete, and status actions through zentao-cli.

faq.question: Should write actions be confirmed?
faq.answer: Yes. The user should confirm the exact object and operation before changes run.

faq.question: Where are credentials stored?
faq.answer: The skill states that zentao-cli caches credentials in the user configuration directory after login.

faq.question: Can it return machine-readable data?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide recommends --format=json when structured output is needed.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
